hypothesis: If crystal material property prediction incorporates cross-modal learning by jointly training graph, text, and X-ray diffraction spectral representations through mutual latent-space prediction with contrastive and variance-invariance-covariance regularization, then the resulting model will achieve lower mean absolute error on benchmark crystal property prediction tasks compared to single-modality baseline models (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN), because integrating complementary structural, semantic, and diffraction information captures richer material representations that individual modalities alone cannot provide.
